en: Pi’i, are you home?
Yes, come on in.
Pi’i, are you home?

ab: ’akia tanasa Pi’i. sua iku ia Kai, ’una kara tia kari? tuturuʉn nakui Pi’i.
ch: B:Pi’i不在家耶!我是Kai，有什麼事嗎? 回來我會轉告Pi’i。
en: Pi’i is not at home. I am Kai. What can I do for you? I will let him know when he comes back.
Who are you?

ab: tamu, sua ikim ia Kini mataa Pi’i. uaranao kim putu’ʉnʉvʉ.
ch: B:奶奶，我們是Kini和Pi’i，幫我們開門。
en: Grandma, we are Kini and Pi’i, please open the door for us.
Who is it?

ab: sua iku ia Pori, tia ku tikirimi Pa’ʉ.
en: I am Pori, I am here for Pa’ʉ.
Grandma, I am here to see you.

ab: miaranau cu kasu koo iavatu. pacʉpʉngʉ ku kasua.
ch: A:你好久沒來了，我好想念你。
en: You haven’t been here for a long time. I missed you so much.
Pi’i, I am here to play with you.

ab: cii! musikaraa! miaranau cu ku maritarʉ kasua.
en: Come on in! I have been waiting for you for a long time.

ab: nimʉkʉa maku sua tammi isi. kutanamaan!
ch: A:這是我種的地瓜，吃吃看!
en: This is the sweet potato I grow. Try it!
The sweet potato is so sweet.

ab: ivataa! nimʉkʉa maku sua macu’u isi, kutanamaan!
ch: A:過來!這是我種的水果，吃看看！
en: Come here! These are the fruits I grow. You should try it.
The fruit tastes sweet.

ab: tupurua, sua isi ia, nimʉkʉa maku yo-ci viaru, kutanamaan!
ch: A:請坐! 這是我種的有機玉米吃看看!
en: Take a seat! You should try some organic corn that I grow.
Wow! The corn tastes so sweet.
Do you want some oranges?

ab: ka’anʉ ku tʉni kumakʉnʉ vʉ’ʉ, kumakʉnʉ kui rinko.
ch: B:我不能吃橘子，我吃蘋果就好。
en: I can’t eat oranges. I will have some apples instead.
Would you want some cookies?
Sure, I like it very much.
Let me peel the fruit for you.
Thank you. This fruit is so juicy.
I will bring you some water.
Thank you.
Enjoy yourself. I cooked a lot of dishes.

ch: B:嗚! 真的很香也好吃！
en: Oh! It smells good and looks delicious.
Enjoy yourself. There is a lot more.
I’ll make myself at home then.
Enjoy yourself. There are plenty here.

ch: B:我吃了好多，不能再吃了！
en: I ate a lot and couldn’t eat any more.
